title: Enterprise Risk Appetite vs Open Critical Vulnerabilities

compliance.assurance_rationale: Auto-migrated from cat-22 markdown on Phase 1.3 run. Requires SME review to confirm clause accuracy, mode (satisfies vs detects-violation-of), and assurance level (contributing/partial/full).

spl:
index=vuln sourcetype=tenable:vuln severity=Critical earliest=-7d
| stats dc(host) as affected_hosts, dc(cve) as open_cves
| append [| inputlookup risk_appetite_thresholds.csv | fields max_critical_hosts | head 1 | eval join=1 ]
| stats max(affected_hosts) as ah max(max_critical_hosts) as lim
| eval breach=if(ah>lim,1,0)
value: Compares counts of open critical findings against a documented risk tolerance threshold so risk management strategy is operationalized, not only slide-deck text.
implementation: (1) Set max_critical_hosts in lookup from risk committee; (2) normalize hostnames; (3) alert on breach=1; (4) document exceptions with compensating controls.
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Vulnerabilities.Vulnerabilities by Vulnerabilities.severity, Vulnerabilities.signature, Vulnerabilities.dest | sort - count
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
